idea_title: Failure Memory for Research Agents for calibration-aware active learning

selected_idea.title: Failure Memory for Research Agents for calibration-aware active learning
selected_idea.pitch: Build and test failure memory for research agents so research agents must connect medical, image, classification, calibration to review and experiment decisions before claims advance.
selected_idea.mechanism: The mechanism is to convert weak natural-language research judgement into explicit state, reviewer, and metric objects. This makes progress falsifiable because an idea must improve a defined local selection or validation metric before it is promoted.

topic: calibration-aware active learning for medical image classification
label: Micro-Probe
hypothesis: Failure Memory for Research Agents for calibration-aware active learning will improve guided idea selection utility over a random baseline under a controlled synthetic research-loop simulation.
method: Simulate noisy research candidates from the run's reviewer and novelty features, then compare evidence-weighted selection against random selection.
dataset: synthetic data generated from current run evidence, idea features, reviewer scores, and novelty risk
expected_result: Guided selection should beat random selection by a positive margin; otherwise the idea is too weak for promotion.
failure_interpretation: A weak or negative margin means the mechanism does not yet justify promotion and should be revised or killed.
